author: ufct
title: Deferred import hides circular dependency between _state and _scoring
notes: Circular dependency is real: _state/filtering.py deferred-imports recompute_stats from _scoring/state_integration.py, while state_integration.py top-level imports path_scoped_issues from _state/filtering.py. This is a genuine layer violation (_state is lower, _scoring is higher). However, deferred imports to break circular dependencies are an extremely common and pragmatic Python pattern — not a design failure per se. The coupling is limited to a single function (remove_ignored_issues) and the fix (dependency injection or moving the function) is straightforward. Moderate concern, well-described but slightly overstated impact.
